denial_pattern_sources: contraindication_to_step_therapy: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; State step-therapy exception laws (NY, TX, IL)
plan_exclusion_overrides_state_mandate: NCSL State Health Insurance Mandates Database 2024; documented in CA, NJ, IL enforcement actions
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 10/24/2026 for R-CHOP cycle continuation related to Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (C83.30). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Cigna Medical Coverage Policies and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG). The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A lower level of care is appropriate for Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (C83.30); R-CHOP cycle continuation exceeds what is required. Outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with level of care too high. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (C83.30). She has been treated under commercial coverage with R-CHOP cycle continuation recommended as the next appropriate step. PET interim Deauville 4; cycle 3 completed without dose-limiting toxicity. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that level of care too high bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
